sections.label: Section 1.1290
sections.original: ἰώ μοι πόνων , ἰὼ Ζεῦ , ἰὼ γᾶ —
sections.translation: ah , for the suffering ! O Zeus , O earth , alas !— a brother ’ s throat , a brother ’ s life , through his shield , through his blood ? Ah me ! ah me ! which of them

sections.label: Section 1.1295
sections.original: τερον ἄρα νέκυν ὀλόμενον ἰαχήσω ;
sections.translation: will I lament as dead ?

sections.label: Section 1.1296
sections.original: φεῦ δᾶ φεῦ δᾶ , δίδυμοι θῆρες ,
sections.translation: Ah , the earth ! Ah , the earth ! Twin savage beasts , two murderous souls with brandished spears will soon be draining the fallen , fallen enemy ’ s blood . Unhappy ,

sections.label: Section 1.1300
sections.original: τάλανες , ὅ τι ποτὲ μονομάχον
sections.translation: that they ever thought of single combat ! In foreign voice I will chant a dirge of tears and wailing , in mourning for the dead . Close to murder stands their fortune ;

sections.label: Section 1.1305
sections.original: κρινεῖ φάος τὸ μέλλον . ἄ-
sections.translation: the coming day will decide it . Fatal this slaughter , fatal , because of the Furies .

sections.label: Section 1307
sections.original: ἀλλὰ γὰρ Κρέοντα λεύσσω τόνδε δεῦρο συννεφῆ
sections.translation: But hark ! I see Creon on his way here to the house with clouded brow , and so I will cease my present lamentations .

sections.label: Section 1310
sections.original: οἴμοι , τί δράσω ; πότερʼ ἐμαυτὸν ἢ πόλιν
sections.translation: Ah me ! what shall I do ? Am I to mourn with tears myself or my city , which has a cloud around it as if it went through Acheron ? My son has died for his country , bringing glory to his name , but grievous woe to me .

sections.label: Section 1315
sections.original: ὃν ἄρτι κρημνῶν ἐκ δρακοντείων ἑλὼν
sections.translation: His body I have just now taken from the dragon ’ s rocky lair and sadly carried the self-slain victim here in my arms ; and the house is filled with weeping ; but now I have come for my sister Jocasta , age seeking age , that she may bathe my child ’ s corpse and lay it out .

sections.label: Section 1320
sections.original: τοῖς γὰρ θανοῦσι χρὴ τὸν οὐ τεθνηκότα
sections.translation: For those who are not dead must reverence the god below by paying honor to the dead .

sections.label: Section 1322
sections.original: βέβηκʼ ἀδελφὴ σή , Κρέων , ἔξω δόμων
sections.translation: Your sister , Creon , has gone out , and her daughter Antigone went with her .

sections.label: Section 1324
sections.original: ποῖ ; κἀπὶ ποίαν συμφοράν ; σήμαινέ μοι .
sections.translation: Where did she go ? What happened ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 1325
sections.original: ἤκουσε τέκνα μονομάχῳ μέλλειν δορὶ
sections.translation: She heard that her sons were about to engage in single combat for the royal house .

sections.label: Section 1327
sections.original: πῶς φῄς ; νέκυν τοι παιδὸς ἀγαπάζων ἐμοῦ
sections.translation: What do you mean ? In my tenderness to my dead son , I was not able to learn this .

sections.label: Section 1329
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οἴχεται μὲν σὴ κασιγνήτη πάλαι ·
sections.translation: It is some time , Creon , since your sister ’ s departure ,

sections.label: Section 1330
sections.original: δοκῶ δʼ ἀγῶνα τὸν περὶ ψυχῆς , Κρέον ,
sections.translation: and I expect the struggle for life and death is already decided by the sons of Oedipus .

sections.label: Section 1332
sections.original: οἴμοι , τὸ μὲν σημεῖον εἰσορῶ τόδε ,
sections.translation: Alas ! I see a sign there , the gloomy look and face of the messenger coming to tell us the whole matter .

sections.label: Section 1335
sections.original: ὦ τάλας ἐγώ , τίνʼ εἴπω μῦθον ἢ τίνας γόους ;
sections.translation: Ah , woe is me ! What story can I tell , what lament can I make ?

sections.label: Section 1336
sections.original: οἰχόμεσθʼ · οὐκ εὐπροσώποις φροιμίοις ἄρχῃ λόγου .
sections.translation: We are lost ; your opening words have no fair appearance .

sections.label: Section 1337
sections.original: ὦ τάλας , δισσῶς ἀυτῶ · μεγάλα γὰρ φέρω κακά .
sections.translation: Ah , woe is me ! I say again ; for I am bringing great horrors .

sections.label: Section 1338
sections.original: πρὸς πεπραγμένοισιν ἄλλοις πήμασιν . λέγεις δὲ τί ;
sections.translation: In addition to the other sorrowful deeds . What is your tale ?

sections.label: Section 1339
sections.original: οὐκέτʼ εἰσὶ σῆς ἀδελφῆς παῖδες ἐν φάει , Κρέον .
sections.translation: Your sister ’ s sons are now no more , Creon .

sections.label: Section 1340
sections.original: αἰαῖ ·
sections.translation: Alas ! you have a great tale of woe for me and the city .

sections.label: Section 1342
sections.original: ὦ δώματʼ εἰσηκούσατʼ Οἰδίπου τάδε
sections.translation: O house of Oedipus , have you heard these tidings of sons slain by the same fate ?

sections.label: Section 1344
sections.original: ὥστʼ ἂν δακρῦσαί γʼ , εἰ φρονοῦντʼ ἐτύγχανεν .
sections.translation: A tale to make it weep , if it were endowed with sense .

sections.label: Section 1345
sections.original: οἴμοι ξυμφορᾶς βαρυποτμωτάτας ,
sections.translation: Oh ! most grievous stroke of fate ! : Alas for my sorrows ! Oh , alas !

sections.label: Section 1347
sections.original: εἰ καὶ τὰ πρὸς τούτοισί γʼ εἰδείης κακά .
sections.translation: If you only know the sorrows other than those !

sections.label: Section 1348
sections.original: καὶ πῶς γένοιτʼ ἂν τῶνδε δυσποτμώτερα ;
sections.translation: How can they be more hard to bear than these ?

sections.label: Section 1349
sections.original: τέθνηκʼ ἀδελφὴ σὴ δυοῖν παίδοιν μέτα .
sections.translation: Your sister has died , with her two sons .

sections.label: Section 1350
sections.original: ἀνάγετʼ ἀνάγετε κωκυ-
sections.translation: Loudly , loudly raise the wail , and with white hands strike upon your heads !

sections.label: Section 1352
sections.original: ὦ τλῆμον , οἷον τέρμονʼ , Ἰοκάστη , βίου
sections.translation: Oh , wretched Jocasta ! what an end to life and marriage you have found the riddling of the Sphinx ! Tell me how her two sons accomplished the bloody deed ,

sections.label: Section 1355
sections.original: ἀρᾶς τʼ ἀγώνισμʼ Οἰδίπου ; σήμαινέ μοι .
sections.translation: the struggle caused by the curse of Oedipus .

sections.label: Section 1356
sections.original: τὰ μὲν πρὸ πύργων εὐτυχήματα χθονὸς
sections.translation: Of our successes before the towers you know , for the walls are not far away so as to prevent your learning each event as it occurred . Now when they ,

sections.label: Section 1360
sections.original: οἱ τοῦ γέροντος Οἰδίπου νεανίαι ,
sections.translation: the young sons of the old Oedipus , had adorned themselves in their bronze armor , they went and took their stand between the armies , chieftains both and two generals for the contest and the single combat . Then Polyneices , turning his eyes towards Argos , lifted up a prayer :

sections.label: Section 1365
sections.original: ὦ πότνιʼ Ἥρα — σὸς γάρ εἰμʼ , ἐπεὶ γάμοις
sections.translation: O Lady Hera , for I am yours , since I have married the daughter of Adrastus and dwell in your land , grant that I may slay my brother , and give my right hand , which is set against him , the victory , stained with his blood . Asking for a shameful crown , to kill his brother .

sections.label: Section 1370
sections.original: πολλοῖς δʼ ἐπῄει δάκρυα τῆς τύχης ὅση ,
sections.translation: And tears came to the eyes of many at their sad fate , and men looked at one another , casting their glances round .

sections.label: Section 1372
sections.original: Ἐτεοκλέης δὲ Παλλάδος χρυσάσπιδος
sections.translation: But Eteocles , looking towards the temple of Pallas with the golden shield , prayed : Daughter of Zeus , grant that this arm may launch the spear of victory

sections.label: Section 1375
sections.original: ἐς στέρνʼ ἀδελφοῦ τῆσδʼ ἀπʼ ὠλένης βαλεῖν
sections.translation: against my brother ’ s breast and slay him who has come to sack my country .

sections.label: Section 1377
sections.original: ἐπεὶ δʼ ἀφείθη πυρσὸς ὣς Τυρσηνικῆς
sections.translation: When the Tuscan trumpet , like a torch , blew the signal for the bloody battle , they darted wildly against one another ;

sections.label: Section 1380
sections.original: κάπροι δʼ ὅπως θήγοντες ἀγρίαν γένυν
sections.translation: like boars whetting their savage tusks , they joined battle , their beards wet with foam . They kept shooting out their spears , but crouched beneath their shields to let the steel glance off in vain ; but if either saw the other ’ s eye above the rim ,

sections.label: Section 1385
sections.original: λόγχην ἐνώμα , στόματι προφθῆναι θέλων .
sections.translation: he would aim his lance there , eager to outwit him with the point .

sections.label: Section 1386
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εὖ προσῆγον ἀσπίδων κεγχρώμασιν
sections.translation: But both kept such careful outlook through the spy-holes in their shields , that their weapons found nothing to do ; while from the onlookers far more than the combatants trickled the sweat caused by terror for their friends .

sections.label: Section 1390
sections.original: Ἐτεοκλέης δὲ ποδὶ μεταψαίρων πέτρον
sections.translation:
1390
Eteocles , in kicking aside a stone that rolled beneath his tread , exposed a limb outside his shield , and Polyneices , seeing a chance of dealing him a blow , aimed at it , and the Argive shaft passed through his leg ;

sections.label: Section 1395
sections.original: στρατὸς δʼ ἀνηλάλαξε Δαναϊδῶν ἅπας .
sections.translation: the Danaid army , one and all , cried out for joy . And the wounded man , seeing Polyneices ’ shoulder bare in this effort , plunged his spear with all his might into his breast , restoring gladness to the citizens of Thebes , though he broke off the spear-head .

sections.label: Section 1400
sections.original: ἐς δʼ ἄπορον ἥκων δορὸς ἐπὶ σκέλος πάλιν
sections.translation: And so , at a loss for a weapon , he retreated step by step , till catching up a splintered rock he let it fly and broke the other ’ s spear in the middle ; and now the combat was equal , for each had lost his lance .

sections.label: Section 1404
sections.original: ἔνθεν δὲ κώπας ἁρπάσαντε φασγάνων
sections.translation: Then clutching their sword-hilts

sections.label: Section 1405
sections.original: ἐς ταὐτὸν ἧκον , συμβαλόντε δʼ ἀσπίδας
sections.translation: they closed , and round and round , with shields clashing , they fought a wild battle . And Eteocles introduced the crafty Thessalian trick , having some knowledge of it from his association with that country . Disengaging himself from the immediate contest ,

sections.label: Section 1410
sections.original: λαιὸν μὲν ἐς τοὔπισθεν ἀμφέρει πόδα ,
sections.translation: he drew back his left foot but kept his eye closely on the pit of the other ’ s stomach from a distance ; then advancing his right foot he plunged the weapon through his navel and fixed it in his spine . Down fell Polyneices , dripping with blood ,

sections.label: Section 1415
sections.original: σὺν αἱματηραῖς σταγόσι Πολυνείκης πίτνει .
sections.translation: ribs and belly contracting in his agony . But the other , thinking his victory now complete , threw down his sword and began to despoil him , wholly intent on that , without a thought for himself . And this indeed tripped him up ; for Polyneices , who had fallen first , was still faintly breathing ,

sections.label: Section 1420
sections.original: σῴζων σίδηρον ἐν λυγρῷ πεσήματι ,
sections.translation: and having in his grievous fall kept his sword , he made a last effort and drove it through the heart of Eteocles . They both lie there , fallen side by side , biting the dust with their teeth , and they have not decided the mastery .

sections.label: Section 1425
sections.original: φεῦ φεῦ , κακῶν σῶν , Οἰδίπου , σʼ ὅσον στένω ·
sections.translation: Ah , ah , how I mourn for your sorrows , Oedipus ! The god , it seems , has fulfilled those curses of yours .

sections.label: Section 1427
sections.original: ἄκουε δή νυν καὶ τὰ πρὸς τούτοις κακά .
sections.translation: Now hear what further woes succeeded . Just as her two sons had fallen and lay dying , their wretched mother came on the scene ,

sections.label: Section 1430
sections.original: σὺν παρθένῳ τε καὶ προθυμίᾳ ποδός .
sections.translation: her daughter with her , in great haste . When she saw their mortal wounds , she wailed : O my sons , the help I bring is too late . And throwing herself on each in turn she wept and mourned , sorrowing over all her toil in nursing them , and their sister , by her side , mourned also :

sections.label: Section 1435
sections.original: στένουσʼ , ἀδελφή θʼ ἡ παρασπίζουσʼ ὁμοῦ ·
sections.translation: Supporters of your mother ’ s age , dearest brothers , leaving me forlorn , unwed ! Then lord Eteocles with one deep dying gasp , hearing his mother , laid on her his clammy hand ,

sections.label: Section 1440
sections.original: φωνὴν μὲν οὐκ ἀφῆκεν , ὀμμάτων δʼ ἄπο
sections.translation: and though he could not say a word , his moistened eye was eloquent to prove his love . And Polyneices was still breathing , and seeing his sister and his old mother he said : Mother , our end has come ; I pity you

sections.label: Section 1445
sections.original: καὶ τήνδʼ ἀδελφὴν καὶ κασίγνητον νεκρόν .
sections.translation: and my sister Antigone and my dead brother . For I loved him though he became my enemy , I loved him in spite of all . Bury me , mother , and you , my sister , in my native land ; pacify the city ’ s wrath that l may get at least that much

sections.label: Section 1450
sections.original: χθονὸς πατρῴας , κεἰ δόμους ἀπώλεσα .
sections.translation: of my own fatherland , although I lost my home . With your hand , mother , close my eyes — he himself places her fingers on the lids — and farewell ; for already the darkness wraps me round .

sections.label: Section 1454
sections.original: ἄμφω δʼ ἅμʼ ἐξέπνευσαν ἄθλιον βίον .
sections.translation: So both at once breathed out their life of sorrow .

sections.label: Section 1455
sections.original: μήτηρ δʼ , ὅπως ἐσεῖδε τήνδε συμφοράν ,
sections.translation: But when their mother saw this sad event , in her overmastering grief she snatched a sword from the dead , and did a fearful deed ; for she drove the steel right through her throat , and there she lies , dead with those she loved so well , her arms thrown round them both .

sections.label: Section 1460
sections.original: ἀνῇξε δʼ ὀρθὸς λαὸς εἰς ἔριν λόγων ,
sections.translation: The army sprang to their feet and fell to wrangling , we maintaining that victory rested with my master , they with theirs ; and there was strife among the generals , some holding that Polyneices gave the first wound with his spear , others that , as both were dead , victory rested with neither .

sections.label: Section 1465
sections.original: κἀν τῷδʼ ὑπεξῆλθʼ Ἀντιγόνη στρατοῦ δίχα .
sections.translation: Meanwhile Antigone crept away from the army . They rushed to their weapons , but by some lucky forethought the people of Cadmus had sat down under arms ; and by a sudden attack we surprised the Argive army before it was fully equipped .
